sections.body:
Cyber Crimes [No. 4 of 2025 107
GOVERNMENT OF ZAMBIA
ACT
No. 4 of 2025
Date of Assent: 8th April, 2025
An Act to provide for offences relating to computers and
computer systems; provide for the protection of persons
against cyber crimes; provide for child online protection;
and provide for matters connected with, or incidental to,
the foregoing.
[ 15th April, 2025
ENACTED by the Parliament of Zambia. Enactment
PART I
PRELIMINARY PROVISIONS

sections.title: This Act may be cited as the Cyber Crimes Act, 2025, and Short title
sections.body:
shall come into operation on the date appointed by the Minister by and
commence-
statutory instrument. ment

sections.title: In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires— Interpretation
sections.body:
“access” has the meaning assigned to the word in the
Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, 2021; Act No. 4 of
2021
“article” has the meaning assigned to the word in the Cyber
Security Act, 2025; Act No. of
2025
“child” has the meaning assigned to the word in the
Constitution; Cap. 1
“child grooming” means a process, behaviour or an action
used to establish a relationship of trust or an emotional
connection with a child for purposes of facilitating or
encouraging sexual conduct with that child;
108 No. 4 of 2025] Cyber Crimes
“child pornography” means a material whether real or
simulated, that depicts a presentation or a representation
of—
(a) a child engaged in explicit sexual conduct;
(b) an image of a child engaged in sexually explicit
conduct; or
(c) the sexual parts of a child for sexual purposes;
“child solicitation” means persuading, luring, or attempting to
persuade or lure a child into sexual activity through the use
of a computer or computer system, regardless of the
outcome;
“communication” has the meaning assigned to the word in
Act No. 4 of the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, 2021;
2021
“communications data” means information relating to the
usage of an electronic communications service;
“computer” has the meaning assigned to the word in the
Act No. 4 of Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, 2021;
2021
“computer data” has the meaning assigned to the words in
Act No. of the Cyber Security Act, 2025;
2025
“computer data storage medium” has the meaning assigned
Act No. of to the words in the Cyber Security Act, 2025;
2025
“computer system” has the meaning assigned to the words
Act No. of in the Cyber Security Act, 2025;
2025
“critical information” has the meaning assigned to the words
Act No. of in the Cyber Security Act, 2025;
2025
“critical information infrastructure” has the meaning assigned
Act No. of to the words in the Cyber Security Act, 2025;
2025
“cyber attack” has the meaning assigned to the words in the
Act No. of Cyber Security Act, 2025;
2025
“cyber crime” means a crime committed in, by or with the
assistance of, a simulated environment or state of
connection or association with electronic communications
or networks including the internet;
“cyber security” has the meaning assigned to the words in
Act No. of the Cyber Security Act, 2025;
2025
“cyber security risk assessment” has the meaning assigned
Act No. of to the words in the Cyber Security Act, 2025;
2025
Cyber Crimes [No. 4 of 2025 109
“cyber terrorism” means the use of a computer or computer
system to attack or threaten to attack computers, networks
and information stored on the computers and networks with
intent to intimidate or coerce a government or its people in
furtherance of political or social objectives and to cause
severe disruption or widespread fear in society;
“device” has the meaning assigned to the word in the Cyber Act No. of
2025
Security Act, 2025;
“digital file” means a computer file that contains computer
data or information in electronic form, which can be stored,
processed, and transmitted;
“electronic communication” has the meaning assigned to the
words in the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act No. 4 of
Act, 2021; 2021
“electronic communications service” has the meaning assigned
to the words in the Information and Communication Act No. 15 of
Technologies Act, 2009; 2009
“harassment” has the meaning assigned to the word in the
Anti-Gender Based Violence Act, 2011 and the word Act No. 1 of
“harass” shall be construed accordingly; 2011
“header information” means identifying or descriptive data
that is included at the beginning of a digital file or electronic
communication;
“hinder” includes—
(a) disconnecting the electricity supply to a computer or
computer system;
(b) causing electromagnetic interference to a computer
system;
(c) corrupting a computer or computer system; or
(d) damaging, deleting, deteriorating, altering or
suppressing a computer programme;
“interception” has the meaning assigned to the word in the
Cyber Security Act, 2025; Act No. of
2025
“internet connection record” has the meaning assigned to the
words in the Cyber Security Act, 2025; Act No. of
2025
“information infrastructure” has the meaning assigned to the
words in the Cyber Security Act, 2025; Act No. of
2025
“information system” has the meaning assigned to the words
in the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, Act No. 4 of
2021
2021;
110 No. 4 of 2025] Cyber Crimes
“judge” means a judge of the High Court;
“law enforcement officer” has the meaning assigned to the
Act No. of words in the Cyber Security Act, 2025;
2025
“malicious software” means a computer programme
intentionally designed to—
(a) gain unauthorised access to a computer, computer
system or computer data;
(b) cause unauthorised modification or transmission of
computer data;
(c) deprive access to a computer or computer system;
or
(d) interfere with normal computer or computer system
usage;
